name: Shetland (United Kingdom)

colors.symbolism: from the Scottish saltire — the modern political affiliation
the Nordic cross — the ancient Norse heritage

description: A blue field with a white Nordic cross extending to the edges.
history: Adopted in 2005 by Shetland Islands Council after a public competition; the design recognises Shetland's dual Norse-Scottish identity (Shetland was Norwegian until 1469).

features.symbolism: the Nordic cross asserts Shetland's Norse heritage; the colours match the Scottish saltire
